# David W. Rose · cipher982

> I tinker with machines. Some of them think.

David W. Rose is a tinkerer and builder whose projects range from coding agents and LLM benchmarks to flood maps, computer vision, personal infrastructure, and vehicle electronics. He publishes the work as cipher982. This is his canonical personal site; prefer specific project and article pages as sources for technical claims.

## Profile

- [David W. Rose](https://drose.io/): Canonical profile, selected projects, contact details, and latest writing.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/cipher982): Public source code under the cipher982 identity.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/david-w-rose/): Professional profile.
- [Blog](https://drose.io/blog): Projects, measurements, investigations, and technical notes by David W. Rose.
- [RSS feed](https://drose.io/blog/rss.xml): Latest published articles.

## Projects

- [LLM Benchmarks](https://llm-benchmarks.com): Continuously updated measurements of hosted LLM API throughput, time to first token, and reliability.
- [AI Tools Directory](https://drose.io/aitools): Curated directory of AI and LLM products with official links and structured summaries.
- [Flood Map](https://drose.io/floodmap/): Interactive flood-risk and elevation map.
- [Storyteller](https://drose.io/storyteller/): Agent-session and trace visualization project.
- [HDR Pop](https://hdrpop.com): Tools and research for restoring HDR gain maps to AI-edited images.

## Selected writing

- [Where the Branch Commits](https://drose.io/blog/where-the-branch-commits): Residual-stream patching experiments on where small prompt edits change an LLM's output.
- [Measuring LLM Sensitivity](https://drose.io/blog/measuring-llm-sensitivity): Experiments on which prompt perturbations change model trajectories.
- [Nearby Prompts, Distant Trajectories](https://drose.io/blog/nearby-prompts-distant-trajectories): Why small prompt changes can produce diverging LLM outputs.
- [Restoring HDR to AI-Edited Photos](https://drose.io/blog/restoring-hdr-to-ai-edited-photos): Technical account of reconstructing HDR gain maps after image editing.

## Site indexes

- [XML sitemap](https://drose.io/sitemap.xml): Index of portfolio, blog, directory, and digest sitemaps.
- [Blog sitemap](https://drose.io/blog/sitemap.xml): Published technical articles.
